criteria: Lexer correctly tokenizes input into WORD, PIPE, REDIRECT_IN, REDIRECT_OUT, REDIRECT_APPEND, REDIRECT_ERR, AMPERSAND, SEMICOLON, NEWLINE, LPAREN, RPAREN, and control flow keywords (IF, THEN, ELSE, FI, WHILE, DO, DONE, FOR, IN)
Lexer handles single-quoted strings as literal (no expansion, backslash is ordinary character)
Lexer handles double-quoted strings with variable expansion ($VAR) and escape sequences (\$, \", \\)
Lexer processes escape character (\) correctly in unquoted context, preserving escaped characters including spaces
Parser constructs a valid AST with node types for SIMPLE_CMD, PIPELINE, LIST, and BACKGROUND
Parser respects operator precedence: pipeline (|) > background (&) > list (;)
Parser generates descriptive error messages for syntax errors with context (e.g., 'syntax error near unexpected token |')
External commands execute via fork + execvp with exit status captured via waitpid
Child process uses _exit(127) not exit() after execvp failure to avoid flushing parent stdio buffers
Built-in commands (cd, exit, export, pwd, unset) execute in shell process without forking
cd changes shell's current directory using chdir() and updates PWD environment variable
export sets environment variables using setenv() and can display current environment when called without arguments
exit terminates the shell with optional status code argument
AST and all associated memory (token strings, argv arrays, redirect targets) are properly freed after execution
Pipeline 'cmd1 | cmd2 | cmd3' creates N-1 pipes, forks N children with concurrent execution, all pipe fds closed in parent and non-participating children, exit status is last command's status
Redirection operators implemented: < (stdin from file), > (stdout truncate), >> (stdout append), 2> (stderr to file), 2>&1 (stderr to current stdout), &> (both stdout and stderr to file)
Redirection order is processed left-to-right; 'cmd > file 2>&1' redirects both to file while 'cmd 2>&1 > file' redirects stderr to original stdout then stdout to file
Variable expansion supports $VAR, ${VAR} syntax, special parameters $? (last exit status), $$ (shell PID), $0 (shell name), $1-$9 (positional parameters)
Tilde expansion converts ~ to $HOME value and ~username to the specified user's home directory via getpwnam()
Glob expansion matches *, ?, and [...] patterns against directory contents; no match returns literal pattern; results are alphabetically sorted
Command substitution $(cmd) executes cmd in subshell, captures stdout via pipe, strips trailing newlines from output, supports nested substitution
Expansion phases execute in correct order: tilde → parameter → command substitution → field splitting → glob → quote removal
Field splitting on IFS (default: space/tab/newline) applies only to results of unquoted expansions
SIGPIPE is handled correctly: producer in pipeline terminates when consumer closes read end
Shell sets SIGINT, SIGTSTP, SIGTTOU, SIGTTIN, and SIGQUIT to SIG_IGN in the shell process itself during initialization
Each pipeline or simple command is placed in its own process group using setpgid(pid, pgid) called in BOTH the parent after fork AND the child before exec to avoid race conditions
Before waiting for a foreground job, the shell calls tcsetpgrp(STDIN_FILENO, job_pgid) to transfer terminal control to the job's process group
After a foreground job completes or stops, the shell calls tcsetpgrp(STDIN_FILENO, shell_pgid) and tcsetattr() to reclaim terminal control and restore terminal modes
When Ctrl+C is pressed, SIGINT is sent by the terminal driver only to the foreground process group; the shell (in its own group) receives no signal and continues
When Ctrl+Z is pressed, SIGTSTP stops all processes in the foreground job's process group; the shell's SIGCHLD handler detects WIFSTOPPED and updates job state to JOB_STOPPED
SIGCHLD handler writes to a self-pipe and returns; all child reaping via waitpid(-1, &status, WNOHANG | WUNTRACED | WCONTINUED) happens in the main loop using only async-signal-safe functions
The SIGCHLD handler reaps children in a loop because multiple children may exit during one signal delivery; the loop continues until waitpid returns 0 or -1
Trailing '&' launches the pipeline in background; the shell prints '[job_id] pgid' and immediately returns to the prompt without waiting
The 'jobs' built-in lists all jobs with format: '[id] state command' where state is Running, Stopped, or Done
The 'fg %N' built-in sends SIGCONT if the job is stopped, calls tcsetpgrp() to give terminal control, and waits synchronously for the job to complete or stop again
The 'bg %N' built-in sends SIGCONT to the stopped job's process group but does NOT transfer terminal control; job remains in background
The shell maintains a job table as a linked list with fields: job_id, pgid, state, command string, is_foreground flag, needs_report flag, and exit_status
Signal blocking via sigprocmask(SIG_BLOCK, ...) protects job table modifications from SIGCHLD interruption
Completed background jobs are reported at the next prompt with '[id] Done command' message before the prompt is displayed
The shell saves terminal modes at startup via tcgetattr() and restores them via tcsetattr() after each foreground job
Signal handlers use only async-signal-safe functions (write, not printf; no malloc, free, or stdio functions)
if/elif/else/fi executes condition command and checks exit status (0=true, non-zero=false) to determine which branch to execute
while cmd; do body; done loops while condition exits 0; until loops while condition exits non-zero
for var in words; do body; done iterates variable over expanded word list; words undergo full expansion (variable, glob, etc.)
Shell functions defined with fname() { body; } syntax; function body executes in current shell process with access to caller's variables
Function arguments accessible as $1 through $9; $0 remains shell/script name; $# gives argument count
return N exits function with status N; return without argument uses last command's exit status
break N exits N enclosing loops (default 1); continue N skips to next iteration of Nth enclosing loop
Script files executed line by line with proper handling of line continuations (trailing backslash)
Comments from # to end of line are ignored; # within quotes is literal
Nested control structures work correctly: if inside while inside for with proper break/continue targeting
Positional parameters for scripts: $1, $2, etc. set from command line arguments
local keyword creates dynamically-scoped local variables in functions; values restored on function exit
Parenthesized commands '(cmd1; cmd2)' execute in a subshell via fork(); variable changes, working directory changes, and environment modifications do not affect the parent shell process
Brace group commands '{ cmd1; cmd2; }' execute in the current shell process without forking; all state changes persist after the group completes
Logical AND '&&' executes the right operand only if the left operand exits with status 0; the entire expression returns the exit status of the last-executed command
Logical OR '||' executes the right operand only if the left operand exits with non-zero status; the entire expression returns the exit status of the last-executed command
Logical operators && and || have equal precedence and left associativity; 'false || true && echo x' executes the echo, 'true || false && echo x' does not

Here-document '<<EOF' reads lines until the delimiter is found at line start, creates a pipe, writes expanded content (if delimiter unquoted) to the pipe, and connects the pipe to stdin before executing the command
Quoted here-document delimiter '<<'EOF'' or '<<\EOF' suppresses variable expansion and command substitution in the here-document body
Here-document with <<- strips leading tabs from content lines and the delimiter, allowing indented here-documents in scripts
Pipeline with subshell '(cmd1; cmd2) | cmd3' correctly forks for the subshell, establishes the pipe, and runs all commands concurrently
Semicolon ';' sequences commands: 'cmd1 ; cmd2' runs cmd1 to completion, then runs cmd2, regardless of either's exit status
'set -e' (errexit) causes the shell to exit immediately when a command exits non-zero, EXCEPT when the command is the left operand of && or ||, part of an if/while/until condition, or negated with !
'set -x' (xtrace) prints each command to stderr before execution, prefixed by PS4 (default '+ ')
'set +e' and 'set +x' disable the respective options
The shell properly handles nested subshells and brace groups at arbitrary depth
Exit status of a subshell is the exit status of the last command executed within it

criteria.description: Lexer tokenizes input into: WORD, PIPE, REDIRECT_IN, REDIRECT_OUT, REDIRECT_APPEND, REDIRECT_ERR, AMPERSAND, SEMICOLON, NEWLINE, LPAREN, RPAREN, IF, THEN, ELSE, FI, WHILE, DO, DONE, FOR, IN
criteria.test: Run test_lexer with all token types; verify each token type is recognized

criteria.description: Parser constructs an AST with nodes for simple commands, pipelines, lists (;), and background (&)
criteria.test: Parse 'ls -la', 'ls | grep', 'echo a; echo b' and verify AST structure matches expected node types

criteria.description: Single-quoted strings are literal (no expansion); double-quoted strings allow $variable expansion but preserve spaces
criteria.test: Tokenize "echo 'hello world'" and verify single argument; tokenize 'echo "hello world"' and verify spaces preserved

criteria.description: Escape character (\) escapes the next character in unquoted and double-quoted contexts
criteria.test: Tokenize 'echo hello\ world' and verify single argument with space

criteria.description: External commands are executed via fork + execvp from the AST; exit status is captured via waitpid
criteria.test: Execute '/bin/true' and verify exit status 0; execute '/bin/false' and verify exit status 1

criteria.description: Built-in commands (cd, exit, export, pwd, unset) are dispatched without forking; cd changes cwd and updates PWD; export sets env vars; student explains WHY these must be built-ins
criteria.test: Run 'cd /tmp' and verify pwd changes; verify no fork for builtin (strace -f shows no clone for cd)

criteria.description: Parser errors produce descriptive messages (e.g., 'syntax error near unexpected token |')
criteria.test: Parse '|' alone and verify error message contains 'unexpected token'

criteria.description: execvp failure in child calls _exit(127) not exit() to avoid flushing parent's stdio buffers
criteria.test: Verify code inspection: child process after execvp failure uses _exit(127)

criteria.description: Memory: No leaks in AST allocation/deallocation cycle
criteria.test: Run valgrind --leak-check=full on test_parser and test_executor; verify 0 leaks

criteria: Lexer tokenizes REDIRECT_IN, REDIRECT_OUT, REDIRECT_APPEND, REDIRECT_ERR tokens
Parser constructs AST with PIPELINE nodes containing multiple commands
Parser constructs REDIRECT nodes with type and target filename
Pipeline executor creates N-1 pipes for N-command pipeline
All pipe file descriptors closed in parent after forking all children
All pipe file descriptors closed in each child except stdin/stdout ends
Pipeline commands run concurrently (not sequentially)
Pipeline exit status is last command's exit status
Redirection < opens file for reading and dup2 to stdin
Redirection > opens file with O_TRUNC and dup2 to stdout
Redirection >> opens file with O_APPEND and dup2 to stdout
Redirection 2> opens file and dup2 to stderr
Redirection 2>&1 calls dup2(stdout_fd, stderr_fd)
Redirection &> opens file and dup2 to both stdout and stderr
Redirections processed left-to-right (order matters for 2>&1)
Variable expansion $VAR expands to environment variable value
Variable expansion ${VAR} equivalent to $VAR
Variable expansion $? expands to last exit status decimal
Variable expansion $$ expands to shell PID decimal
Variable expansion $1-$9 expand to positional parameters
Tilde expansion ~ expands to HOME environment variable
Tilde expansion ~user expands to user's home from getpwnam
Tilde expansion only at word start (not mid-word)
Command substitution $(cmd) executes cmd in subshell
Command substitution captures stdout via pipe
Command substitution strips trailing newlines from output
Command substitution supports nested $(echo $(date))
Field splitting divides expansion results on IFS characters
Field splitting default IFS is space, tab, newline
Glob expansion * matches any sequence of characters
Glob expansion ? matches any single character
Glob expansion [abc] matches any character in set
Glob expansion [!abc] or [^abc] negates character class
Glob expansion * does not match leading dot (dotfiles)
Glob no match returns pattern literally (POSIX behavior)
Glob matches sorted alphabetically
Quote removal strips single and double quotes from final words
Expansion phases execute in order: tilde, parameter, cmdsub, field, glob, quote
Double quotes prevent field splitting and glob but allow variable expansion
Single quotes prevent all expansion
Shell ignores SIGINT, SIGTSTP, SIGTTOU in the shell process itself
Each pipeline is placed in its own process group using setpgid() called in BOTH parent and child to avoid race
Foreground job's process group is given terminal control via tcsetpgrp(STDIN_FILENO, job_pgid) before waiting
Shell reclaims terminal control via tcsetpgrp(STDIN_FILENO, shell_pgid) after foreground job completes or stops
Ctrl+C sends SIGINT only to the foreground job's process group; shell is unaffected
Ctrl+Z sends SIGTSTP to the foreground job's process group; shell updates job state to Stopped and shows prompt
SIGCHLD handler reaps background processes with waitpid(-1, ..., WNOHANG) using async-signal-safe functions only
Trailing '&' launches pipeline in background; shell prints '[N] PID' and returns prompt
'jobs' lists all jobs with number, state (Running/Stopped/Done), and command
'fg %N' sends SIGCONT if stopped, gives terminal control, and waits for completion/stop
'bg %N' sends SIGCONT to stopped job, leaves it in background
Completed background jobs are reported at next prompt
Lexer tokenizes if/then/else/elif/fi/while/until/for/in/do/done keywords
Parser constructs AST for if/elif/else/fi with elif as nested if in else_body
Parser constructs AST for while/until loops with condition and body
Parser constructs AST for for loops with variable, word list, and body
execute_if runs condition command and branches on exit status (0=true)
execute_while loops while condition exits 0; execute_until loops while condition exits non-zero
execute_for expands word list and iterates, setting variable for each word
break [N] uses longjmp to exit N loop levels; error if not in loop
continue [N] uses longjmp to skip to next iteration; error if not in loop
Function definitions stored in hash table; body is AST (not string)
Function calls save/restore positional parameters ($1-$9)
return [N] exits function with status N; error if not in function
local var[=value] creates scoped variable; restored on function exit
Script execution handles shebang, comments, line continuation
Control flow constructs nest correctly (if in while in for, etc.)

criteria.description: Parse (compound_list) syntax into NODE_SUBSHELL AST
criteria.test: Parser accepts (echo hello) and produces NODE_SUBSHELL

criteria.description: Execute subshell with fork isolation, state changes don't propagate
criteria.test: (cd /tmp) leaves cwd unchanged in parent

criteria.description: Subshell exit status propagates to parent
criteria.test: (exit 42); echo $? prints 42

criteria.description: Parse { compound_list; } syntax into NODE_BRACE_GROUP AST
criteria.test: Parser accepts { echo hello; }

criteria.description: Execute brace group without fork, state changes persist
criteria.test: { cd /tmp; }; pwd shows /tmp

criteria.description: Parse cmd1 && cmd2 with equal precedence to ||
criteria.test: true || false && echo test parses as ((true || false) && echo)

criteria.description: Execute && with short-circuit on left failure
criteria.test: false && echo no produces no output

criteria.description: Parse cmd1 || cmd2 with equal precedence to &&
criteria.test: false || true parses correctly

criteria.description: Execute || with short-circuit on left success
criteria.test: true || echo no produces no output

criteria.description: Parse <<EOF and read content until delimiter
criteria.test: cat <<EOF\nhello\nEOF produces WORD(hello)

criteria.description: Quoted delimiter suppresses variable expansion
criteria.test: cat <<'EOF'\n$HOME\nEOF outputs $HOME literally

criteria.description: <<- strips leading tabs from content and delimiter
criteria.test: cat <<-EOF\n\tindented\n\tEOF outputs 'indented'

criteria.description: Here-document provided via pipe to command stdin
criteria.test:
wc -l <<EOF
a
b
c
EOF outputs 3

criteria.description: set -e exits shell on command failure
criteria.test: set -e; false; echo never exits without printing

criteria.description: set -e suppressed in && and || operands
criteria.test: set -e; false || true continues executing

criteria.description: set -e suppressed in if/while conditions
criteria.test: set -e; if false; then :; fi; echo done prints done

criteria.description: set -x prints commands before execution
criteria.test: set -x; echo hello prints + echo hello to stderr
